page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs. The processor includes advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate vector and matrix operations.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across compute-intensive tasks. In single-core and multi-core Geekbench tests, the server scored 2142.0 and 17695.0 respectively, placing it in the strong category. Image processing and text processing benchmarks achieved top-tier status, with scores of 18505.0 and 2733.0. The server also demonstrates robust capabilities in cryptography and compression, achieving 31.98 GB/sec in single-threaded gzip compression and 277.22 GB/sec in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is measured at 3206.74 GB/sec for cached small blocks, though it drops to 150.28 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

Given its high compute-to-memory ratio and lack of local storage, this server is designed for workloads that require heavy CPU processing but have moderate memory footprints. It is highly efficient for tasks like software compilation, image processing, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference, where its AMX instructions provide a significant performance boost. The qualitative cost profile is highly favorable for compute-bound workloads, offering high resource density and specialized instruction sets without the overhead of excess memory or local storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 24 physical cores with hyper-threading and a 260 MB L3 cache. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, focusing its resource density entirely on raw CPU performance. Benchmark data highlights strong capabilities, with top-tier performance in image and text processing, alongside strong results in cryptographic operations, compression, and static web serving. It also features Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix math. This configuration is highly efficient for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, CPU-based machine learning inference, and high-throughput web serving, offering a cost-effective balance of high-performance processing power and moderate memory capacity.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized server with 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and advanced AMX instruction support, delivering strong performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for high-throughput processing and CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8581C processors
96.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core ratio)
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix math
Top-tier performance in image and text processing benchmarks
Optimized for software compilation, CPU-based inference, and web serving
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
